$comment: Phase C cutover criteria for GECR rules in soak mode (#1276/#1291 convention). Each rule's soak_window + deny_flip_trigger codifies when the warn→deny mode flip is required. Closes the a72d712 (#1415) / caef8ab (#1437) meta-recurrence pattern: 'rule ships warn-only → soak window passes without follow-up → advisory-only carve-out becomes permanent → enforcement gap silently re-introduced'. The verify-linter rule `gecr-cutover-overdue` (template-coherence-rules.json) flags any entry whose soak_window elapsed AND flip_pr_required is still true.

recovery-path-skip-pairing.deny_flip_trigger: At least 1 candidate of kind=recovery-path-skip emitted AND correctly filed-or-suppressed in a real /verify or /change run

sparse-trace-pairing.deny_flip_trigger: At least 1 sparse-trace candidate (init-stub-survived OR lead-orchestrated-missing-aoc-v1.3) detected AND paired with an observation in a real run

prior-failure-consultation-gate.$comment: Step 14c gate in verify-recurrence-guard.py — enforces prior_failure_consultation[] for dossier entries with designer_consultation_attestation_required=true. Tracked alongside the two GECR rules above so the same gecr-cutover-overdue linter rule (template-coherence-rules.json) flags it when overdue, even though it is a verifier-side gate rather than a declarative GECR rule.
prior-failure-consultation-gate.deny_flip_trigger: At least 1 /solve --defect run with semantic-match dossier entry AND consultation correctly emitted
